$comment: SITE CONFIG — the one file to fill in with Caitlin's real details.
IMPORTANT: any social handle left as "" is HIDDEN from the page entirely.
We never ship a dead icon. The old site had four social icons all pointing at href="#",
which did nothing and threw a console error. Empty = gone, not broken.
There is deliberately NO phone and NO email. Caitlin does not want bookings,
emails, or inbound messages. Every route to her goes through Instagram.
name: Caked by Caitlin
$tagline_comment: This is the visible headline under the hero photo, and it IS the page's <h1>.
The name and the 'Curated by' kicker used to sit here and were removed on purpose:
the hero shot has CAKEDBYCAITLIN printed on every card scattered across the floor,
so setting the name in type right underneath it just said the same thing twice.
The brand name is still in the <h1> for crawlers and screen readers (visually hidden),
because search is Caitlin's only inbound channel and an <h1> of just 'Shop my favs'
tells Google nothing about who she is.
bio: Wedding hair & makeup artist. These are the products I actually use on my brides — nothing in here is a guess.
$hero_comment: Square, not a circle. Loads in black and white; click it and it turns to colour, same as the wall of her work.

$comment: Full profile URLs. Leave blank to HIDE that icon — we never ship a dead link.
The Instagram handle is @cakedbycaitlin, which is printed on the business cards
scattered across the floor of the hero photo. Confirm the others with Caitlin.

storefronts.$comment: Her external shops, rendered as their own large cards. Blank = hidden.

$authBase_comment: The Cloudflare Worker that handles 'Sign in with GitHub'. ONE worker serves BOTH the
/admin CMS and the in-place photo editor (?edit=1) — same OAuth app, same worker.
Leave it blank and the editor simply says 'Editing is not set up yet' instead of failing
with an opaque DNS error. Setup is ~10 minutes: see docs/LOGINS.md.

$comment: Caitlin has NO booking form and NO email — so search is her only inbound channel,
and it has to land on the shop and earn through affiliate links.
Keyword first, brand last: that's how you rank for anything other than your own name.
seo.title: Caked by Caitlin | Wedding Makeup Artist's Product Edit
seo.description: The exact products a working wedding makeup artist uses on her brides — skin prep, lips, powders, blush and tools. Shop the edit.

$comment: MUST render above the first affiliate link on EVERY page that has one.
A footer-only disclosure is not legally sufficient (FTC 16 CFR 255.5).
The Amazon line is mandated verbatim by the Associates Operating Agreement — do not reword it.
disclosure.general: The products featured here may contain affiliate links — I earn a commission if you buy through them, at no extra cost to you.
disclosure.amazon: As an Amazon Associate I earn from qualifying purchases.
